description: Business-domain MOLT Use blocks. Use answers: 'What is this for?' Each block describes the business function or purpose of the Subject — what that thing does or enables in a commercial context.
usage: Select the Use block that best describes what the business Subject is being used for. Use anchors the reasoning chain between Subject and Aim in business contexts.

SALES_REVENUE.description: Uses related to generating and closing revenue

blocks.function: To identify and attract potential buyers into the sales funnel
To determine which leads have genuine buying potential and fit
To advance an opportunity through defined pipeline stages toward close
examples: moving a deal from proposal to negotiation
advancing a prospect past security review

blocks.function: To obtain a formal commitment and signed agreement from the buyer
To increase revenue from existing customers by expanding scope, seats, or tiers
examples: expanding a contract to new business units

MARKETING_DEMAND.description: Uses related to building awareness, demand, and brand

blocks.function: To create and reinforce a consistent identity and reputation in the market
To create market interest that produces a pipeline of qualified opportunities
To position an offering against alternatives and communicate its value to target buyers
To deploy coordinated marketing activities across channels to a defined audience
To keep existing customers engaged and reduce churn through ongoing communication

CUSTOMER_SUCCESS.description: Uses related to onboarding, retaining, and growing customers

blocks.function: To bring a new customer to first value quickly and correctly
To track customer engagement and risk signals to guide proactive intervention
examples: monitoring support ticket volume per customer

blocks.function: To secure contract renewal before expiry through proactive engagement
examples: initiating a renewal conversation 90 days out

blocks.function: To resolve critical customer issues with urgency and accountability
examples: convening an emergency response team for a churning account

blocks.function: To collect systematic feedback that informs product and service improvement
examples: facilitating a customer advisory board session

FINANCIAL_MANAGEMENT.description: Uses related to managing, reporting, and controlling financial performance

blocks.function: To allocate available financial resources across departments and initiatives
To track, reconcile, and communicate revenue performance against plan
To monitor and reduce expenditures to protect margin and efficiency
To ensure the business has sufficient liquidity to meet its obligations
To project future revenue, costs, and profitability based on current trends and plans
examples: scenario planning for a board presentation

OPERATIONS_EXECUTION.description: Uses related to running day-to-day business operations efficiently

blocks.function: To run established business processes consistently to deliver repeatable outcomes
To assign people, equipment, and time to activities to maximize utilization
examples: scheduling field technicians to service calls
allocating consulting hours to client projects

blocks.function: To ensure the organization has the right resources to meet anticipated demand
examples: headcount plan for a new product launch
support team capacity for a seasonal peak

blocks.function: To govern relationships with external suppliers to ensure quality and compliance
examples: running a quarterly vendor business review
evaluating a supplier against SLA commitments

blocks.function: To verify that products and services meet defined standards before delivery

HUMAN_RESOURCES.description: Uses related to attracting, developing, and retaining people

blocks.function: To identify, attract, and hire the right people for open roles
examples: posting a job and managing applications

blocks.function: To integrate new hires into the organization so they can contribute quickly
examples: running a new hire orientation program
assigning a buddy and 30/60/90 day plan

blocks.function: To set expectations, provide feedback, and evaluate employee contributions
To build employee skills and knowledge to support growth and retention
examples: providing access to an online learning library

blocks.function: To design and administer pay structures and benefits that attract and retain talent
examples: running the annual compensation review cycle

STRATEGY_PLANNING.description: Uses related to setting direction and making high-level decisions

blocks.function: To define multi-year direction, priorities, and resource allocation for the business
examples: running the annual strategic planning process
setting OKRs for the business unit

blocks.function: To understand the competitive landscape and position the business advantageously
examples: tracking competitor pricing and GTM moves

blocks.function: To gather intelligence about customer needs, market trends, and opportunity size
examples: running a buyer persona research study

blocks.function: To build the financial and strategic rationale for a proposed investment or initiative
examples: writing an investment memo for a new product
presenting the ROI case for a technology purchase

blocks.function: To evaluate and prioritize initiatives across the business to maximize return on investment
examples: scoring and ranking the product roadmap

PRODUCT_MANAGEMENT.description: Uses related to defining, building, and evolving products

blocks.function: To sequence and communicate what will be built and when based on strategy and capacity
examples: publishing an external roadmap to customers
reprioritizing the roadmap after a market shift

blocks.function: To translate customer needs and business goals into specific product requirements
examples: defining user stories for a sprint
specifying acceptance criteria for a feature

blocks.function: To test product ideas with users before committing to full development
examples: releasing a beta to early adopters
A/B testing a new onboarding flow

blocks.function: To bring a new product or feature to market in a coordinated, effective way
examples: coordinating a GA launch across product, marketing, and sales
staging a beta to GA rollout
preparing launch enablement materials for sales

blocks.function: To measure how a product is performing against goals and user expectations
examples: analyzing support tickets for product pain points

LEGAL_COMPLIANCE.description: Uses related to managing legal obligations, risk, and regulatory compliance

blocks.function: To draft, review, execute, and maintain commercial agreements
examples: negotiating and signing a customer MSA
tracking contract obligations and expiry dates

blocks.function: To ensure the business meets its legal and regulatory obligations
examples: maintaining GDPR compliance for customer data

blocks.function: To secure and defend the business's proprietary assets
To identify, assess, and mitigate legal and operational risks
examples: managing litigation risk with outside counsel
implementing a data breach response plan

blocks.function: To ensure internal policies are understood, applied, and monitored consistently
examples: running an annual policy acknowledgment process
auditing compliance with an expense policy

PARTNERSHIP_ALLIANCES.description: Uses related to building and operating strategic business relationships

blocks.function: To identify, attract, and sign new business partners into a partner program
examples: reaching out to a target reseller
presenting a channel partner value proposition

blocks.function: To equip partners with the knowledge, tools, and support to sell and deliver effectively
examples: delivering technical enablement to a SI partner
providing co-marketing materials to a reseller

blocks.function: To work jointly with a partner on shared opportunities to increase win rates
examples: teaming on a joint prospect call
registering a deal for partner co-sell

blocks.function: To manage the health, obligations, and evolution of strategic partnerships over time
examples: running a quarterly partner business review
tracking mutual commitments in a strategic alliance

blocks.function: To grow the network of integrated and complementary offerings around a platform
examples: building a marketplace of partner integrations
enabling ISVs to build on a platform

REPORTING_ANALYTICS.description: Uses related to measuring, reporting, and deriving business insight

blocks.function: To deliver accurate, timely performance summaries to leadership and the board
To monitor key performance indicators against targets on a regular cadence
To transform business data into actionable insight through analysis and visualization
To project future business outcomes based on current data and trends
examples: sales forecast for the current quarter

blocks.function: To review what happened after an outcome to learn and improve
examples: win/loss analysis after a competitive deal
churn analysis after a customer departure
retrospective after a missed quarterly target

CHANGE_MANAGEMENT.description: Uses related to managing organizational transitions and adoption

blocks.function: To restructure teams, roles, and reporting lines to better fit strategy
examples: shifting from functional to product-led teams

blocks.function: To inform stakeholders about changes in a way that builds understanding and reduces resistance
examples: communicating a policy change to employees
managing external messaging around a pivot

blocks.function: To drive uptake of new tools, processes, or behaviors across the organization
examples: rolling out a new CRM to the sales team
driving adoption of a new performance management process
migrating an organization to a new communication platform

blocks.function: To bring all relevant parties to shared understanding and commitment to a change
examples: running a pre-reorg alignment series with leaders
engaging key influencers before a process change

blocks.function: To build the skills and knowledge employees need to operate effectively after a change
examples: delivering new tool training to all users
creating a self-serve learning path for a new process

PROCUREMENT_SUPPLY.description: Uses related to sourcing, acquiring, and managing goods and services

blocks.function: To identify and evaluate potential suppliers for a required good or service
examples: running an RFP for a new software platform
shortlisting vendors for a consulting engagement

blocks.function: To complete the purchasing process from approval to receipt
examples: raising and approving a purchase order
completing a procurement intake for a new tool

blocks.function: To track, analyze, and optimize expenditure across categories
To measure and manage vendor delivery against contractual commitments
examples: conducting a quarterly SLA review with a supplier
scoring a supplier on a performance scorecard

blocks.function: To oversee the flow of goods and materials from source to delivery
examples: managing inventory levels and reorder points
tracking inbound shipments against delivery commitments

CUSTOMER_ACQUISITION.description: Uses related to the full motion of bringing new customers into the business

blocks.function: To divide a market into distinct groups that can be targeted with tailored approaches
examples: defining ICP segments by industry and size
identifying underserved niches in an addressable market

blocks.function: To initiate contact with potential customers who have not yet expressed interest
examples: running a cold email sequence to a target list
making outbound calls to a prospect list
using LinkedIn for direct outreach to decision-makers

blocks.function: To convert website visitors or content consumers into identified leads
examples: gating a whitepaper to capture contact info
converting a free trial signup to a discovery call
following up with a webinar attendee

blocks.function: To build a commercial offer that demonstrates fit and value for a specific prospect
examples: writing a custom SOW for a services deal
preparing a product demo with tailored ROI analysis

blocks.function: To let a prospect validate the product in their environment before committing
examples: running a paid pilot with evaluation criteria
executing a POC with defined success metrics

BRAND_COMMUNICATIONS.description: Uses related to external and internal communications that shape perception

blocks.function: To manage the organization's reputation and media presence
examples: pitching a product launch story to press
building relationships with key industry analysts

blocks.function: To produce and distribute valuable content that attracts and educates target buyers
examples: publishing a thought leadership blog series

blocks.function: To maintain and grow the brand's presence and engagement on social platforms
examples: responding to customer mentions on social

blocks.function: To keep employees informed and engaged with organizational news and direction
examples: managing an internal intranet news section

blocks.function: To build brand, generate pipeline, and engage customers through live and virtual events
examples: sponsoring and staffing an industry trade show

RISK_GOVERNANCE.description: Uses related to identifying, managing, and reporting on business risk

blocks.function: To surface and catalogue threats to business objectives before they materialize
examples: stress-testing a financial forecast for downside scenarios

blocks.function: To reduce the likelihood or impact of identified risks through proactive action
examples: diversifying a supplier base to reduce concentration risk

blocks.function: To plan, execute, and respond to internal or external audits of the business
To ensure the business can operate through and recover from disruption
To set and enforce behavioral standards across the organization
examples: running an ethics and compliance training program

INVESTOR_RELATIONS.description: Uses related to managing capital, investors, and board relationships

blocks.function: To raise capital from investors to fund growth or operations
examples: running a Series B fundraising process
preparing pitch materials for a VC roadshow

blocks.function: To keep current investors informed of business performance and material developments
To prepare for and facilitate effective board governance and decision-making
examples: preparing and distributing a board deck
managing board committee charters and calendars
following up on board action items

blocks.function: To assess, negotiate, and close acquisitions or mergers
examples: running a buy-side due diligence process
structuring and negotiating an acquisition agreement

blocks.function: To decide how to deploy available financial resources across competing priorities
examples: prioritizing R&D investment vs. sales headcount
deciding between organic growth and acquisition

TALENT_CULTURE.description: Uses related to building and maintaining organizational culture and employer brand

blocks.function: To shape and reinforce shared values, behaviors, and norms across the organization
examples: recognizing behaviors that reflect cultural ideals

blocks.function: To maintain and improve staff commitment, motivation, and satisfaction
examples: designing a recognition and rewards initiative

blocks.function: To shape how the organization is perceived as a place to work by candidates and employees
examples: publicizing employee stories and culture content

blocks.function: To create a workplace where people of all backgrounds can contribute and thrive
examples: auditing hiring and promotion data for bias

blocks.function: To ensure the organization has the right skills and capacity for future needs
examples: identifying critical skills gaps for a three-year plan
building a succession plan for key roles
planning for a geographic expansion hiring wave

GROWTH_EXPANSION.description: Uses related to scaling the business and entering new markets

blocks.function: To launch the business or a product into a new geographic or vertical market
examples: expanding into a new industry vertical
launching a new customer segment GTM motion

blocks.function: To establish operations, sales, or delivery in a new location
examples: hiring a country manager for a new market
establishing local entity and compliance for a new geography

blocks.function: To grow revenue by adding adjacent offerings to the existing portfolio
examples: adding a new product module to a platform

blocks.function: To build new routes to market that increase reach and reduce cost of sales
To grow the number of paying customers by targeting new segments or demographics
examples: launching an SMB motion alongside an enterprise play
building a consumer offering alongside a B2B one

UMG_BUSINESS_USE.description: Uses of UMG cognitive architecture within business and commercial contexts

blocks.function: To constrain an AI sales agent to approved messaging, pricing, and escalation protocols
examples: ensuring an AI SDR uses only approved talk tracks
routing enterprise deals to human AEs

blocks.function: To govern AI support agents so they stay within policy and escalate correctly
examples: constraining a support bot to documented resolutions only
requiring human handoff for billing disputes
enforcing tone and compliance standards in AI responses

blocks.function: To run a governed AI that analyses business data and surfaces insights within defined bounds
examples: an AI analyst that reports only on verified data
a pipeline review AI constrained to approved metrics

blocks.function: To automate compliance checking with auditable governance and full trace
examples: policy adherence checking with Decision Trace
regulatory filing review with governance pass

blocks.function: To provide governed AI reasoning support for high-stakes business decisions
examples: a governed strategic analysis agent for leadership
a board report generation agent with auditability
a merger evaluation AI with traceable recommendation logic
